images.alt:
A painterly fantasy scene unfolds within a dark, tunnel-like forest framed by twisting black branches that arch overhead like the walls of a cathedral. At the center stands a small child with short dark hair and oversized, expressive eyes. The child wears a simple dark coat and boots, appearing tiny against the immense spectacle surrounding them. Their face is softly illuminated by a glowing heart-shaped light resting in one hand, casting warm golden tones onto their cheeks.
Above the child, a magnificent swirl of luminous white and pale blue energy sweeps across the scene in the shape of a dragon's head and neck. The dragon appears formed from frost, feathers, mist, and brushstrokes of light rather than solid scales. Its glowing presence curves protectively around the child, creating an archway that dominates the composition.
Dozens of origami-like birds and cranes emerge from the dragon's flowing form. Some are bright white, others dark silhouettes, all suspended in motion as they circle through the icy air. Tiny golden embers drift among them like fireflies. The contrast between warm orange light and cool winter blues creates a sense of wonder and mystery.
In the distance, faint towers and rooftops rise from a misty cityscape, their windows glowing softly through the haze. Frost-covered grasses and crystalline textures frame the foreground, while sweeping brushwork gives the entire image the feeling of a dream painted in light, ice, and memory. The scene balances loneliness and comfort, with the child appearing both fragile and protected beneath the radiant flock and spectral dragon.
Keywords:
fantasy, dragon spirit, child, glowing heart, origami birds, winter forest, dreamscape, frost, magical light, painterly, wonder, enchanted
AI-generated; use is permitted for non-commercial creative projects. MidJourney, FLUX.1-schnell model via Perchance, Artspace.ai, REVE, Echoform™.

A low-angle portrait captures a person standing beneath a canopy of translucent golden fabric that billows dramatically overhead. The camera looks upward from chest level, making the figure appear immersed in a whirlwind of sunlight, silk, and floating flower petals. Their head is tilted back slightly, eyes closed, with a peaceful smile suggesting contentment and freedom. Short-cropped hair catches the light, while vivid yellow eye makeup echoes the warm tones surrounding them.
Layers of gauzy fabric sweep across the frame from every direction, creating a sense of movement and immersion. Golden petals drift through the air like confetti, suspended in bright afternoon light against a pale blue sky. The fabric forms soft arcs and folds above the subject, almost resembling sunlit clouds or banners caught in a summer breeze.
The person wears flowing yellow and cream-colored garments gathered around the neck and shoulders. Tattoos are visible along the neck, arms, and hands, adding contrast and texture against the soft fabrics. The image is filled with warm amber and sunflower hues, while shallow depth of field turns foreground fabric and petals into luminous blurs. The overall effect feels joyful, airy, and radiant, as though the subject is standing inside a living burst of sunshine and wind.
Keywords:
golden portrait, yellow fabric, flower petals, sunlight, windblown cloth, tattooed subject, low angle, joyful expression, warm tones, summer atmosphere, flowing motion, contemporary fashion

A dramatic gothic seascape unfolds beneath a sky of cold gray mist and storm-dark clouds. On the far left edge of a towering sea cliff stands the solitary figure of a woman dressed in a long black Victorian-style gown. Her body is turned in profile toward the right side of the image, facing a distant lighthouse barely visible through the fog. Wind pulls at her dark hair and trailing garments, giving her an ethereal, almost spectral appearance.
The most striking feature of the composition is the way the lower portion of her dress dissolves into a swirling eruption of crimson autumn leaves. The leaves scatter outward across the image, as though her form is slowly transforming into windblown fragments of memory. Bright embers of warm red, orange, and pale gold glow among the leaves, creating a vivid contrast against the otherwise cool palette of blue-gray fog and dark ocean water.
Below the cliff, rough waves churn around jagged black rocks. White foam gathers in narrow channels and crashes against the stone formations, emphasizing the restless energy of the sea. In the distance, a tall lighthouse emerges faintly through the haze, illuminated by a soft shaft of pale light that pierces the fog. The structure appears dreamlike and uncertain, as though it exists somewhere between reality and recollection.
Painterly brush textures cover the entire image, blending edges into mist and atmosphere. The scene feels suspended between mourning and hope, with the distant beacon serving as a quiet point of guidance amid the storm.
Keywords:
gothic lighthouse, autumn leaves, spectral woman, stormy sea, coastal cliff, foggy horizon, Victorian dress, painterly fantasy, windswept figure, ocean mist, melancholic atmosphere, dark romance

I am lounging on a velvet chaise longue in the center of the room, surrounded by a diverse group of friends who are laughing, dancing, and conversing in a blurred background. I am wearing an oversized, crisp white men’s dress shirt, unbuttoned slightly, with silk pajama pants. My expression is soft and knowing, a subtle smile playing on my lips as I gaze directly at the camera. To infuse the queer energy, there are subtle touches—two figures in the background are gently holding hands, a leather jacket draped casually over a chair, a rainbow flag sticker on a guitar case in the corner. The film grain is visible and rich. The vibe is timeless, intimate, and celebrates the beauty of ordinary moments with your chosen family.

images.alt:
More so than most of his predecessors, Trump strained against the limits of the presidency. He never understood that the office was bound by higher law — that his power wasn’t absolute. He never understood that he was an officer of the Constitution and a servant of the people; he never understood that he was a subject and not the sovereign.

It was because of this fundamental misunderstanding — itself tied to his bottomless solipsism — that Trump tried to twist and turn the presidency into an extension of his ego. To the extent he failed to accomplish this, it was only because he was stymied by those around him — officials who chose to honor their commitment to the Constitution over the interests of one man. Those same officials now warn that if he is given another term in office, he will rule as a tyrant.

America got lucky. It won’t get lucky again. Free of the guardrails that kept him in place the first time, affirmed by the Supreme Court and backed by allies and apparatchiks in the conservative movement, Trump will merge the office of the presidency with himself. He will shake it from its moorings in the Constitution and rebuild it as an instrument of his will, wielded for his friends and against his enemies. In doing so, he will erode the democratic assumptions that undergird our current constitutional order. And he will have the total loyalty of a Republican Party that itself is twisting and abusing the counter-majoritarian features of the American system to undermine and unravel democracy in the states it controls.

value.text: - @jamellebouie.net’s column not long before his reelection on how a second Trump era would likely mean the end of the American republic as we knew it (he didn’t mean 🇺🇸 literally wouldn’t exist; the U.S. has gone thru multiple republics already) has rung in my brain for the past year and a half
Good, necessary... also blindingly obvious and a bit belated. God I hope Bouie writing it for NYT can help get it through the thick skulls of the oldest, most powerful Democrats though.

images.alt:
You can think of this Trump administration as a similar state of affairs. The American people broke something when they gave Trump a second chance in office. And there is no going back to the Union as it was. If Democrats hope to lead the nation to any kind of recovery, much less renewal, they must understand and internalize this fact of the matter.

Broken eggs cannot be mended. To try to do so, to try to return to some notion of normality is to court failure. Worse, it is to play a repeat of the last Democratic administration when, in pursuit of the familiar, the Democratic Party all but passed the baton back to reactionaries working toward something revolutionary.

images.alt:
COLUMN:
INTERNET RESOURCES
AI LITERACY RESOURCES
Annis Lee Adams
University Libraries, California State University - East Bay, Hayward, CA

We know students are using generative AI to brainstorm research topics, to develop research questions, and to find sources for research projects in addition to using AI for writing papers and creating presentations. We also know that, at this time, AI tools have limited ability to find high-quality, relevant sources; frequently hallucinate citations that do not exist; and provide no grounding or context for why they present sources or where they found them.

Teaching students and our academic colleagues AI literacy is imperative. AI literacy includes discussing the strengths and limits of generative AI tools by explaining how they operate, how they may be biased, and how they are different from traditional search engines, such as Google and library databases. Furthermore, AI literacy includes guiding students to create productive, iterative prompts and evaluating AI outputs. Additionally, students need to understand appropriate, ethical, and responsible uses of AI tools.

images.alt:
COLUMN: ECHNOLOGY
Brooke Gross
University Libraries, Western Kentucky University, Bowling Green, KY

AI literacy long term: 
Conversations and implications
While there is a great deal of research and discussion on the efficacy, pitfalls, and various uses of artificial intelligence in academic settings, it seems there is very little emphasis on the long-term consequences of delegating thought-based tasks or how information professionals in particular can engage in meaningful and productive conversations surrounding these risks. This article will examine the potential impacts of increased reliance on artificial intelligence and identify ways in which libraries can lead AI literacy initiatives from a place of neutrality, utilizing existing information literacy and critical thinking expertise.

#SpecialLibraries #SpecialCollections

images.alt:
Simple and sustainable: Maintaining the Merry-Go-Round of exhibitions at The Grolier Club
Shira Belén Buchsbaum
In September 2026, The Grolier Club will open our 1,000th and 1,001st exhibitions since our founding in January 1884. These two exhibitions reflect some of the foundational aspects of The Grolier Club’s exhibition’s program: observing, narrating, and interpreting the history of book making; celebrating practitioners and innovators; and expanding notions on what constitutes the art and history of the book. The oldest and largest bibliophilic membership society in the United States, the Grolier Club is proud that over 142 seasons and three buildings in Manhattan, we have always hosted exhibitions of books and prints open to the public free of charge. This paper reflects on that rich history, our ever-evolving approach, and how we continue to develop exhibitions that further our mission “to celebrate the art and history of the book.”

images.alt: A breathtaking photorealistic render of a contemporary sculptural bust, displayed on a dark circular base against a warmly blurred golden sunset background. The sculpture depicts a female figure with a dramatically elongated neck and simplified shoulders. The skull is formed as a near-perfect dark sphere of brushed oxidized steel — its surface etched with thousands of fine parallel lines giving it a textile-like quality. The sphere is cut open in a curved window, revealing the face nestled within: a serene, idealized female visage with eyes closed in peaceful contemplation, its surface textured with crackled amber, rust and burnt gold — as if lit from within by an inner flame. From the neck downward, elegant ribbon-like metal flanges and spiral bands sweep outward in fluid curving arcs, wrapping and unwinding around the form like a slow cyclone frozen in time. The interplay between cool charcoal-grey outer surfaces and warm glowing amber inner tones creates a powerful visual tension. The bokeh background of orange and gold clouds completes the sunset atmosphere. Museum-quality sculptural art, octane render, fine art photography of sculpture.
